title: Minimal Test Scene
description: A 3-step scene used to verify the Phase 4 simulation page layouts, ControlBar, and ChallengesSection.

explanation.heading: Step 0 — Initial State
explanation.body: The array starts with values [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. No elements are highlighted. This is the initial state before any search operation begins.

explanation.heading: Step 1 — Highlight Middle
explanation.body: We highlight the **middle element** (index 2, value 3) as the current focus of our search. In Binary Search, this is the pivot point.
explanation.callout: Index 2 is now active — check if it matches the target.

explanation.heading: Step 2 — Found!
explanation.body: The last element (index 4, value 5) is marked as **found**. The search has successfully located the target value.
explanation.callout: Target located at index 4. Search complete!

challenges.title: Predict the next step
challenges.description: If we continue past step 2 with a different target value, what element would be highlighted next in a Linear Search?

challenges.title: Break it: duplicate values
challenges.description: What happens if the array contains duplicate values [1, 2, 2, 4, 5]? Which index gets returned first in a Linear vs Binary search?

challenges.title: Optimize the search
challenges.description: The array is already sorted. Can you reduce the number of comparisons needed to find element 5? Switch to Binary Search mode above and count the steps.

challenges.title: Real-world scenario
challenges.description: Imagine this array represents sorted user IDs in a database. Which search algorithm would you use for production lookups and why?
